description_long: This factory building features a long, rectangular main structure with a blue gabled roof and red brick lower walls, topped by three prominent smokestacks and a large central vent. The facade is lined with rows of large industrial windows, providing ample natural light to the interior. Attached to the main building is a secondary, slightly smaller brick structure with a matching blue roof, connected by a series of pipes and ducts. The exterior displays a utilitarian industrial aesthetic, with visible rooftop machinery and metal tanks adjacent to the building. Loading dock doors are present at ground level, indicating freight and vehicle access. The overall design is characteristic of early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture, emphasizing function and durability.
description: A large, rectangular factory building with blue gabled roofs, red brick walls, multiple smokestacks, and rows of large industrial windows. Attached is a secondary brick structure, with visible rooftop machinery and loading dock doors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with three smokestacks, large industrial windows, blue roof, and loading dock doors at ground level.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the secondary brick structure, visible piping, and the connection between the two buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the building, extensive rows of industrial windows, and the attached secondary structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gabled roof, smokestacks, and minimal window openings.
